D10 Imaging L2 Understand Reference

Sunny 16 Rule

on a sunny day, set aperture to f/16 and shutter to 1/ISO — every other condition is a stop adjustment from there

The mnemonic: in bright direct sunlight, exposure is correct at aperture f/16 and shutter 1/ISO seconds. At ISO 100 that's f/16 · 1/100; at ISO 400 that's f/16 · 1/400. Every other condition is a relative stop adjustment — open one stop (f/11) for "slight overcast", two for "overcast", three for "heavy overcast", four for "open shade".

Cycle the lighting condition and ISO. The recipe panel shows the implied aperture / shutter pair. The exposure preview shows the same scene at correct exposure across all conditions — same brightness, different settings.
